```{=html}
<!-- Modal -->
<div class="modal fade" id="facet-early-definition"data-bs-keyboard="false" tabindex="-1"  aria-hidden="true">
  <div class="modal-dialog modal-dialog-scrollable">
    <div class="modal-content">
      <div class="modal-header">
        <h1 class="modal-title fs-5">Summarize</h1>
        <button type="button" class="btn-close" data-bs-dismiss="modal" aria-label="Close"></button>
      </div>
      <div class="modal-body">
```

Summaries of data returned from an API request have a special term called "facet" which has a special meaning when dealing with Crossref's REST API, which we will cover in later parts of this tutorial. If you want to skip to that section, check out the glossary entry [here^{{< fa arrow-right-to-bracket >}}^](/blog/CR_API_Tutorials/glossary.qmd#query)

```{=html}
      </div>
      <div class="modal-footer">
        <button type="button" class="btn btn-secondary" data-bs-dismiss="modal">Close</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```
